title: MAPriors: Browser-Based Meta-Analytic Predictive Priors and Dynamic Borrowing Engine
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can meta-analytic predictive priors and dynamic borrowing be made accessible through a browser-based tool without requiring R or Stan programming expertise? Eight historical Crohn disease placebo-arm studies totaling 960 patients from the Neuenschwander canonical dataset were analyzed alongside oncology and cardiovascular built-in examples. MAPriors, a single-file HTML application of 2,565 lines, implements Bayesian hierarchical modeling with robust mixture priors, power priors, and commensurate priors, along with effective sample size computation via the Morita method. The Crohn dataset produced a prevalence MAP prior mean of 17.7% (95% credible interval 11.2-25.8%) with an effective sample size of 959 patients under near-zero heterogeneity. Operating characteristics simulation confirmed nominal type I error near 5% and power gains of 10 to 15 percentage points over vague-prior designs. This is the first browser-based MAP prior engine, validated by 38 Selenium tests, democratizing regulatory-grade Bayesian borrowing. A limitation is that the implementation uses approximate Gibbs sampling rather than full Hamiltonian Monte Carlo inference.

sentences.text: Can meta-analytic predictive priors and dynamic borrowing be made accessible through a browser-based tool without requiring R or Stan programming expertise?
Eight historical Crohn disease placebo-arm studies totaling 960 patients from the Neuenschwander canonical dataset were analyzed alongside oncology and cardiovascular built-in examples.

MAPriors, a single-file HTML application of 2,565 lines, implements Bayesian hierarchical modeling with robust mixture priors, power priors, and commensurate priors, along with effective sample size computation via the Morita method.
The Crohn dataset produced a prevalence MAP prior mean of 17.7% (95% credible interval 11.2-25.8%) with an effective sample size of 959 patients under near-zero heterogeneity.
Operating characteristics simulation confirmed nominal type I error near 5% and power gains of 10 to 15 percentage points over vague-prior designs.
This is the first browser-based MAP prior engine, validated by 38 Selenium tests, democratizing regulatory-grade Bayesian borrowing.
A limitation is that the implementation uses approximate Gibbs sampling rather than full Hamiltonian Monte Carlo inference.

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.
